📊 Executive Overview

High-level KPIs and trends across the movie dataset.

📋 Executive Summary

🔍 Key Findings

Avg rating: 64.9/100 across 9,660 movies. Revenue: $2475.00B. Top genre: Drama. Production peak: 2022 (941).

🚀 Opportunities

Highest-rated genre: Music (70.6). Hidden gems: 745 films with score ≥75, low budget.

⚠️ Risks

Worst-rated: Unknown (54.4). Budget-revenue correlation moderate (r≈0.69). ~25% of high-budget films fail to recoup.

📌 Recommendations

Portfolio: 20% blockbuster, 30% mid-budget, 30% genre, 20% niche. Quality gate: IMDb 60+.

Rating Distribution
IMDb score distribution with mean/median
Year Trends
Production volume and avg rating over time (1950+)
Revenue Analysis
Revenue distribution and budget relationship
💡 Key Insight

Average ratings have stayed near 63/100 for decades despite a 50x production increase.

🎯 Why It Matters

This disproves the "quality dilution" hypothesis — more movies have not meant worse movies.

📌 Recommendation

Focus on production pipeline efficiency. Mid-budget films ($20M–$60M) offer best risk-adjusted returns.

Genre Distribution
Top 15 genres by volume vs avg rating
💡 Key Insight

Drama dominates production; Music achieves highest ratings (70.6).

🎯 Why It Matters

Genre selection is the most impactful portfolio decision. ANOVA confirms genre significantly predicts ratings (p<0.001).

📌 Recommendation

Allocate 30% high-volume, 30% high-quality, 20% high-revenue, 20% experimental genres.

Top 20 Movies
Highest-rated movies in the dataset
💡 Key Insight

Top score: 100. Top 20 span multiple genres — no single category dominates.

🎯 Why It Matters

Critical acclaim is genre-agnostic. Quality can be achieved in any category with the right execution.

📌 Recommendation

Do not restrict greenlighting by genre. Apply consistent quality standards across all projects.

Budget vs Rating
Rating vs budget, size=revenue, color=genre
💡 Key Insight

Budget shows negligible correlation with ratings (r≈0.06). High spending ≠ high quality.

🎯 Why It Matters

Many high-budget films underperform critically. The best investments are modest-budget productions.

📌 Recommendation

Implement budget oversight tied to creative benchmarks. Stage-gate funding reduces large-production risk.

Genre Distribution
Top 15 genres by volume vs avg rating
💡 Key Insight

Best: Music (70.6). Worst: Unknown (54.4).

🎯 Why It Matters

Genre has significant impact on ratings (ANOVA p<0.001). Genre choice is a strategic, not just creative, decision.

📌 Recommendation

Balance prestige genres (Documentary, Biography) with commercial genres (Action, Animation).

Revenue by Genre
Revenue by genre (log scale, top 12)
Rating Distribution
IMDb score distribution with mean/median
💡 Key Insight

Highest median revenue and highest ratings are not aligned — different genres lead each metric.

🎯 Why It Matters

Revenue and ratings must be tracked independently. A genre can excel at one while failing at the other.

📌 Recommendation

Use a dual-metric scoring system (rating × revenue) to evaluate genre performance holistically.

Machine learning pipeline: clustering, PCA, and segmentation.

📖 Data Storytelling — The Complete Narrative

1. What does the dataset contain?

9,660 movies from 1903 to 2023 across 20 genres and 59 countries. Cleaned to 9,660 records after removing 518 duplicates.

2. What are the overall trends?

Production surged from less than 100/year pre-1980 to 941 at peak (2022). Average ratings stable at ~65/100 — no quality dilution.

3. What drives movie success?

Budget weakly correlates with ratings (r≈0.06). Genre and storytelling matter far more than spending. Mid-budget films ($20M–$60M) offer the best risk-adjusted returns.

4. What should stakeholders do?

Diversify portfolio: 20% blockbuster, 30% mid-budget quality, 30% genre plays, 20% niche. Implement quality gate (IMDb 60+). Use genre history for data-driven greenlighting.

IMDb Movies Analytics Dashboard — Portfolio Project
9,660 movies · 20 genres · 59 countries · 1903–2023
Built with Python · Pandas · Scikit-learn · Plotly
Pipeline: Cleaning → EDA → Feature Engineering → ML → Dashboard
